facts: Aspen Aerogels reported Q1 2025 revenue of $78.7 million, a 17% year-over-year decline that fell short of the $82.74 million forecast.
The company posted a Q1 2025 EPS of -$0.06, missing the forecasted -$0.05, with net income of -$301,200 (-$3.67 per diluted share).
Gross profit for Q1 2025 was $22.8 million, down 35% from the previous year, with EV thermal barrier gross margins at 23% against a 35% target.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2025 was $4.9 million, supported by an adjusted OpEx run rate of $25.8 million.
Aspen Aerogels holds a cash position of $192 million and reduced debt by over $20 million in Q1, including $13.2 million toward its revolving credit facility and $6.5 million to MidCap.
Total debt on facilities stood at $141.8 million at the end of Q1 2025.
The company provided a Q2 2025 revenue outlook of $70 to $80 million, with adjusted EBITDA expected between breakeven and $7 million.
Aspen Aerogels is targeting a revenue breakeven point for adjusted EBITDA at approximately $245 million and aims to achieve $90 million in adjusted EBITDA at $360 million in revenue.

Q1 2025 CapEx was $13 million, with $7.7 million allocated to remaining obligations for Plant 2 and the remainder for equipment in Mexico and Rhode Island linked to future EV thermal barrier launches.
The company expects to spend up to an additional $20 million to finish demobilizing its Georgia facility and close out remaining open obligations.
Aspen Aerogels is selling equipment from its Statesboro, Georgia site to selected buyers before holding an auction, with the plant available for purchase through a broker.
Q4 2024 included a $286.6 million impairment of Plant 2 assets in Statesboro, Georgia, and $9.8 million in restructuring and financing expenses linked to the plan.
EV thermal barrier revenue fell 25% year-over-year to $48.9 million in Q1 2025 as demand aligns with lower vehicle production schedules at key customers.
The company secured a major pyrofin award from GM for a next generation prismatic EV platform, following recent awards from Mercedes Benz and Volvo Trucks.
Energy Industrial business revenue increased 2% year-over-year to $29.8 million in Q1 2025, with the company targeting full-year revenue approximating last year's $145.9 million.

Aspen Aerogels has operations on both sides of U.S.-China trade lines and caps its total 2025 tariff exposure on raw materials to less than $4.5 million, potentially under $1 million by switching silanes and batting sourcing to U.S. suppliers.

Energy Industrial products are included in Annex II, exempting them from additional duties levied on imports announced on April 2, keeping total tariffs/duties at about 50% versus roughly 174% for non-exempt products.
Finished thermal barrier parts fabricated in Mexico are USMCA compliant and currently exempt from tariffs, utilizing a maquila setup to avoid trade duties or value added taxes.
The company is proactively addressing potential tariff risks through pricing strategies and working with its external manufacturing facility (EMF) to lower product costs.
CEO Don Young stated that electrification through this decade will be a major driver for both the thermal barrier and energy industrial businesses.
CFO Ricardo Rodriguez noted the company is on a path to put adjusted EBITDA on the green line, targeting at least $20 million in annualized adjusted EBITDA on revenues as low as $250 million.

New OEM awards tied to realistic growth expectations are expected to drive over $200 million in additional revenue by 2027, with EV thermal barrier segment potential reaching over $700 million in 2027.
The company's stock dropped 25.48% in premarket trading following the earnings release, falling to $4.24 per share near its 52-week low of $4.8.
Management will host one-on-one investor meetings on Wednesday, May 21 at the B. Riley Securities 25th Annual Investor Conference.
Fixed cost reductions were implemented in mid-February 2025 to streamline the company and lower the revenue level required for positive adjusted EBITDA performance.
The EV thermal barrier business faces pressures from evolving battery pack designs, declining content per vehicle, and smaller battery pack size mixes affecting revenue streams.
Aspen Aerogels maintains a maquila setup in Mexico that enables temporary inventory imports, value addition, and duty-free exports to customers for vehicle or battery pack assembly.
The company's aerogel manufacturing plant is located in East Providence, Rhode Island, with raw material sourcing optimized across Asia, Europe, and the United States.
Analysts from Barclays, Craig Hallum, and Oppenheimer participated in the earnings call to inquire about the Georgia facility monetization strategy and tariff impacts.
